LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS MICHELE D AMICO AND SIDNEA D AMICO'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT ORDERS THAT PLAINTIFFS ARE RESTORED TO THEIR PRIOR POSITIONS WITH SOUTH BEACH CAFE, INC. AND DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM INTERFERING WITH THE MANAGEMENT OF THE BUSINESS UNLESS PLAINTIFFS ARE REMOVED FROM THEIR OFFICES IN COMPLIANCE WITH THE CORPORATIONS CODE. PLAINTIFFS SHALL POST A $25,000 BOND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CCP 529. (ORDER SIGNED AFTER HEARING.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: A R G U E D. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFFS MICHELE D AMICO AND SIDNEA D AMICO'S MOTION TO DETERMINE VALIDITY OF ELECTION OF CORPORATE OFFICERS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFFS' PRESENT EVIDENCE, INCLUDING TAX RETURNS, THAT INDICATE THEY HAVE AN OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN SOUTH BEACH CAFE, INC. THE EVIDENCE ALSO DEMONSTRATES THAT THE MEETING THAT OCCURRED ON FEBRUARY 20, 2014, DID NOT COMPORT WITH CALIFORNIA CORPORATIONS CODE SECS. 602 AND 709. THE PARTIES WERE INSTRUCTED TO RETURN TO DEPARTMENT 302 ON MONDAY, APRIL 7, 2014 AT 9:30 AM FOR REASSIGNMENT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR# 5976.

Plaintiff'S Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers

Plaintiffs Michele D Amico And Sidnea D Amico'S Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers
Off Calendar Apr-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. RON LUNSKI OF THE LUNSKI LAW, TEL. NO. 426-3525 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. ERIC L. TOSCANO OF THE TOSCANO KELOGG & VAN AKEN LLP, TEL. NO. 1-888-235-8582 EXT. 101 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - Plaintiffs Michele D Amico And Sidnea D Amico'S Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers IS TRANSFERRED TO DEPT. 206 FOR REASSIGNMENT TO ANOTHER JUDGE. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ron Lunski, Esq. of Lunski Law, Tel. 415.426.3525, for plaintiff and moving party;
Eric L. Toscano, Esq. of Toscano Kellogg & Van Aken LLP, Tel. 888.235.8582 x101, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS MICHELE D AMICO AND SIDNEA D AMICO'S Motion To Determine Validity Of Election Of Corporate Officers is granted. Plaintiffs' present evidence, including tax returns, that indicate they have an ownership interest in South Beach Cafe, Inc. The evidence also demonstrates that the meeting that occurred on February 20, 2014, did not comport with California Corporations Code secs. 602 and 709. The parties were instructed to return to Department 302 on Monday, April 7, 2014 at 9:30 am for reassignment. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Apr-04-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ron Lunski, Esq. of Lunski Law, Tel. 415.426.3525, for plaintiff and moving party;
Eric L. Toscano, Esq. of Toscano Kellogg & Van Aken LLP, Tel. 888.235.8582 x101, for defendant and opposing party.

RULING: A R G U E D. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFFS MICHELE D AMICO AND SIDNEA D AMICO'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is granted in part. The Court orders that plaintiffs are restored to their prior positions with South Beach Cafe, Inc. and defendant is enjoined from interfering with the management of the business unless plaintiffs are removed from their offices in compliance with the Corporations Code. Plaintiffs shall post a $25,000 bond in compliance with CCP 529. (Order signed after hearing.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
